summary: The sync-react workflow failed when attempting to push a new React canary sync branch to the repository. The `nextjs-bot` account was denied push access to `vercel/next.js` with a 403 error, indicating the token used lacks the necessary permissions to push to the repository.
root_cause: The GITHUB_TOKEN or token used by `nextjs-bot` does not have push permission to `vercel/next.js`. The git push command returned exit code 128 with stderr: 'remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.' and HTTP 403.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicit GitHub remote rejection message showing the bot account was denied push access.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: HTTP 403 Forbidden confirms the token used for authentication lacks push permissions.
Identifies the exact command that failed — the push of the React sync branch.
Exit code 128 from git is consistent with a remote authentication/permission failure.

suggested_fix: Ensure the token provided to `nextjs-bot` (passed as the `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var to the sync-react step) has `Contents: write` permission and push access to `vercel/next.js`. If a PAT is used, verify it has not expired and is scoped with `repo` or at minimum `public_repo` write access for the nextjs-bot account.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
